one_line_takeaway: Representation Rerouting reroutes a model's harmful-output representations to orthogonal directions, delivering large attack-agnostic robustness gains (avg ASR ~77%->10% on Mistral, ~38%->4% on Llama-3, 0.8% for Cygnet) against unseen text, image, and agent attacks with under 1% capability loss.
key_finding: Circuit-breaking with RR generalizes strongly to unseen attacks while barely touching capability. Average attack success rate falls from 76.7% to 9.8% on Mistral-7B and from 38.1% to 3.8% on Llama-3-8B (an 87% and 90% relative reduction respectively), with MT-Bench and OpenLLM scores essentially preserved (<1% dip), whereas the adversarially trained R2D2 Mistral baseline loses over 8% MT-Bench and still averages 31.7% ASR. The Cygnet finetune drives average ASR to 0.8% (roughly two orders of magnitude below refusal training) while IMPROVING capability. The same method cuts multimodal PGD-attack compliance by 84% and reduces agent harmful-action compliance by 83-84%, in both cases with near-zero capability loss.
core_contribution: The paper introduces 'circuit breakers' for LLMs, and a concrete instantiation called Representation Rerouting (RR). Rather than removing specific attack vulnerabilities (adversarial training) or supervising outputs (refusal/RLHF/DPO), RR directly reroutes the model's internal representations that give rise to harmful generations toward orthogonal/incoherent directions, so that when the model begins producing harmful content its own forward pass is short-circuited. This is attack-agnostic by construction (it targets the harmful-output representations, independent of which attack elicited them) and requires no adversarial fine-tuning or auxiliary guard model. RR is implemented with LoRRA (LoRA adapters, from the representation-engineering line of work) and a cosine-similarity rerouting loss plus an L2 retain loss. The authors demonstrate it on text LLMs (Mistral-7B, Llama-3-8B), a multimodal model (LLaVA-NeXT-Mistral-7B), and AI agents doing function calling, and ship a stronger Llama-3-8B finetune called Cygnet. This is the paper's OWN contribution; RepE [77] and the RMU unlearning method [29] are cited prior work that RR builds on and generalizes.
threat_model: An adversary who wants to elicit generically harmful content (content the model should never produce) from an aligned model, bypassing its safeguards. The paper evaluates against a broad, deliberately unseen (not trained against) attack suite spanning black-box jailbreaks (PAIR, TAP-Transfer, AutoDAN, HumanJailbreaks/Manual, multilingual), white-box gradient attacks (GCG), system-level prefilling, and worst-case white-box access via input-embedding-space and representation-space (RepE) attacks. For multimodal models the threat is white-box image 'hijacks' via PGD (epsilon 32/255, 1000 steps). For agents the threat is direct harmful function-call requests and 'forced function calling' (assistant forced to begin with a chosen function name, analogous to prefilling). The paper is explicit that it targets ONLY the 'produce-harmful-content' aim; it does not defend traditional label-flipping adversarial attacks where no output is inherently harmful.
limitations: Stated by the authors: (1) the method only defends the specific adversarial goal of producing generically harmful content; it would NOT defend a VLM used as a drop-in image classifier against label-flipping attacks, since no class label is inherently 'harmful.' (2) The work focuses on single-turn conversations. (3) The Harmfulness Probing comparison is run under a weaker threat model where the attacker does not know about or optimize against the probe. Reviewer-observed: the Circuit Breaker Set is synthetic and the category-ablation (Figure 5) shows robustness is sensitive to how well the training distribution covers the target harm category (narrow categories like cybercrime generalize worse), so coverage of the harmful-output set is the practical dependency. Even the strongest configuration leaves nonzero residual ASR under the unrealistically strong input-embedding attack (e.g., 15.7% Mistral, 9.6% Llama, 7.9% Cygnet).

datasets_used: HarmBench (harmful behaviors + attack suite, robustness eval)
133 harmful multimodal behaviors sourced from HarmBench + MM-SafetyBench
New 100-request agent function-calling safety benchmark (cybercrime, disinformation, fraud, harassment)
OpenLLM Leaderboard suite: MMLU, ARC-c, HellaSwag, TruthfulQA, Winogrande, GSM8K
Berkeley Function Calling Leaderboard / BFCL (agent capability)
quantitative_results: Mistral-7B-Instruct-v2 average ASR over attack suite: Refusal Trained 76.7%, Adv Trained R2D2 31.7%, +RR 9.8% (87% relative reduction)
Llama-3-8B-Instruct average ASR: Refusal Trained 38.1%, +RR 3.8%, Cygnet 0.8% (90% relative reduction for RR)
MT-Bench capability: Mistral RT 7.60 / R2D2 6.00 / +RR 7.53; Llama-3 RT 8.05 / +RR 8.00 / Cygnet 8.21
OpenLLM score: Mistral RT 65.4 / R2D2 61.2 / +RR 65.4; Llama-3 RT 68.8 / +RR 68.3 / Cygnet 71.9
Per-attack ASR reductions (Llama-3 RT -> +RR): GCG 44.5->2.5, Prefilling 84.9->3.3, Input Embed 80.4->9.6, RepE Attack 91.2->8.7; Cygnet reaches 0.0 on all listed attacks except Input Embed (7.9)
Multimodal PGD attack: RR reduces compliance rate by 84% vs original model and 85% vs safety prompt; MMMU/LLaVA-Wild within 0.5% of original (safety prompt loses 3.3% on LLaVA-Wild)
AI agents: RR reduces harmful-action compliance by 84% (direct request) and 83% (forced function-calling) vs baseline, retaining BFCL performance
Harmfulness Probing (Table 2) average ASR: Mistral RT 80.6 -> HP-Linear 19.0 / HP-MLP 14.3 / RR 11.2; Llama-3 RT 32.6 -> HP-Linear 9.0 / HP-MLP 6.8 / RR 3.1 (RR best, MLP probe closest)
PGD config: epsilon = 32/255, 1000 optimization steps; circuit breaking applied at Llama-3 layers 10 and 20
baselines_compared: R2D2 adversarially-trained Mistral model (SFT-only, trained against GCG) from HarmBench
Harmfulness Probing with a linear classifier (representation-reading alternative)
Harmfulness Probing with a 2-layer MLP classifier (hidden 64/32)
RR loss ablations: RMU random-vector loss, RandP and RandC random-positive/centered variants, with/without refusal-retain and with/without circuit-breaker augmentation
